title: hieracium echioides subsp. procerum
intro: Hieracium echioides subsp. procerum, also known as the hawkweed or false hawkbit, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating gastrointestinal issues and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and roots are said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of digestive problems, wounds, and eczema, offering relief from pain and promoting healing. In herbal preparations, Hieracium echioides subsp. procerum is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its effectiveness and safety as a medicinal herb have not been extensively scientifically studied.
